articles.headline: India Declares Three-Day State Mourning Following Demise of Pope Francis
articles.explainer:
• The Central Government announced a three-day period of state mourning across India from April 21st to 23rd, 2025, as a mark of respect for the passing of His Holiness Pope Francis.
• Pope Francis, the head of the Catholic Church and sovereign of the Vatican City State, passed away at the age of 88.
• During this period, the National Flag will fly at half-mast on all government buildings nationwide, and there will be no official entertainment.
• The declaration underscores India's respect for global spiritual leaders and its diplomatic relations with the Vatican, a significant global institution.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighbourhood- relations
Important International institutions, agencies and fora
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: Duration of state mourning declared by India.
Significance of state mourning as a diplomatic gesture.
Role of Pope Francis as head of Catholic Church and Vatican City State.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of India declaring state mourning for a global religious leader in the context of its soft power and diplomatic outreach.
Analyze the historical and contemporary relevance of India's relations with the Vatican and its role in international interfaith dialogue.
Examine the protocols and implications of state mourning in India and internationally.
articles.imageDescription: Indian National Flag flying at half-mast over a government building.
articles.trivia: The Vatican City is the smallest independent state in the world, both in area and population.

articles.headline: Veteran Politician Tilakdhari Singh Passes Away at 87
articles.explainer:
• Tilakdhari Singh, a veteran politician and former Member of Parliament, passed away at the age of 87.
• Known for his dedicated service to public life, Singh had a long and impactful career spanning several decades.
• He was recognized for his contributions to rural development and social justice initiatives in his constituency.
• His demise marks the end of an era for regional politics, with many leaders expressing condolences.
gsSubTopics: Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these.
Role of political parties in a democracy.
prelimsPoints: Tilakdhari Singh's political career and key roles held.
General awareness of significant political personalities.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolving role of veteran politicians in contemporary Indian democracy.
Analyze the impact of individual political leaders on regional development and social policies.
articles.trivia: The average tenure of a Member of Parliament (Lok Sabha) is five years.

articles.headline: India Unveils National Cyber Security Strategy 2025 Focusing on Critical Infrastructure Protection
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) launched the National Cyber Security Strategy (NCSS) 2025.
• The strategy emphasizes enhanced protection for critical information infrastructure (CII) across sectors like energy, finance, and telecommunications.
• Key pillars include strengthening cyber resilience, fostering innovation in cyber technologies, and developing a skilled cybersecurity workforce.
• It aims to promote public-private partnerships and international collaboration to combat rising cyber threats.
gsSubTopics: Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges, basics of cyber security
Money-laundering and its prevention.
articles.source: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY)
prelimsPoints: Implementing agency of NCSS 2025.
Key focus areas of NCSS 2025 (e.g., CII protection, skill development).
Importance of international collaboration in cybersecurity.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the provisions of the National Cyber Security Strategy 2025 and its potential effectiveness in safeguarding India's digital assets.
Discuss the challenges in protecting Critical Information Infrastructure from state-sponsored and non-state cyber actors.
Evaluate the role of public-private partnerships in enhancing national cybersecurity capabilities.
articles.imageDescription: Digital representation of interconnected networks and a shield symbol.
articles.trivia: India ranks among the top 10 countries most targeted by cyberattacks globally.

articles.headline: RBI Expands Digital Rupee (e₹) Pilot to Include More Banks and Use Cases
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) announced a significant expansion of its Central Bank Digital Currency (CBDC) pilot project for both wholesale (e₹-W) and retail (e₹-R) segments.
• The expansion will include additional public and private sector banks and explore new use cases, such as cross-border payments and programmable payments.
• The move aims to gather more comprehensive data on the operational efficiency, technological resilience, and user adoption of the digital currency.
• This initiative is part of RBI's broader strategy to modernize India's payment infrastructure and enhance financial inclusion.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: Purpose of RBI's Digital Rupee pilot project.
Distinction between wholesale and retail CBDC.
Potential new use cases for Digital Rupee (e.g., cross-border, programmable payments).
mainsPoints: Assess the potential benefits and challenges of a Central Bank Digital Currency (CBDC) in India, particularly concerning financial stability and monetary policy transmission.
Discuss how the Digital Rupee could impact traditional banking systems and digital payment ecosystems in India.
Examine the technological underpinnings of CBDC and its implications for data privacy and cybersecurity.
articles.imageDescription: Digital representation of Indian Rupee symbol with blockchain elements.
articles.trivia: Globally, over 100 countries are exploring or developing their own CBDCs.

articles.headline: ISRO Begins Preparations for Chandrayaan-4 Mission, Eyeing Lunar Sample Return
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) has initiated preliminary work for its ambitious Chandrayaan-4 mission, slated for launch in the late 2020s.
• This mission aims to achieve a lunar sample return, bringing back precious lunar soil and rock samples for scientific analysis on Earth.
• Chandrayaan-4 is envisioned as a multi-module mission, potentially involving a lander, rover, and an ascent vehicle to collect and return samples.
• This mission builds upon the successes of Chandrayaan-1, Chandrayaan-2, and Chandrayaan-3, further solidifying India's capabilities in lunar exploration.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of Space.
prelimsPoints: Primary objective of Chandrayaan-4 (lunar sample return).
Sequence of Chandrayaan missions (1, 2, 3, 4).
Key components of a sample return mission.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of lunar sample return missions for scientific research and national prestige in space exploration.
Analyze the technological challenges ISRO might face in executing a lunar sample return mission and its implications for future deep-space endeavors.
Evaluate India's position in global space exploration, considering its lunar missions and future ambitions.
articles.imageDescription: Artistic rendering of a spacecraft collecting samples on the lunar surface.
articles.trivia: Only three countries (USA, Russia/USSR, China) have successfully conducted lunar sample return missions so far.

articles.headline: Government Initiates Consultations for New National Health Policy Aimed at Universal Healthcare
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare has begun nationwide consultations for a new National Health Policy (NHP).
• The proposed policy aims to consolidate gains from previous policies and address emerging health challenges, including non-communicable diseases and pandemic preparedness.
• Key focus areas include strengthening primary healthcare, integrating digital health solutions, and ensuring equitable access to quality healthcare for all citizens.
• Stakeholders from medical professionals, public health experts, civil society, and state governments are invited to contribute.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
articles.source: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
prelimsPoints: Ministry responsible for the National Health Policy.
Key objectives of the new NHP (e.g., universal healthcare, NCDs, digital health).
Stakeholders involved in the consultation process.
mainsPoints: Evaluate the effectiveness of previous National Health Policies in India and discuss the imperative for a new policy in the current socio-economic and epidemiological context.
Analyze the challenges in achieving universal healthcare access in India, particularly concerning funding, infrastructure, and human resources.
Discuss the role of digital health solutions and primary healthcare strengthening in improving health outcomes across the country.
articles.imageDescription: Doctors and patients in a modern primary healthcare center.
articles.trivia: India's first National Health Policy was formulated in 1983.

articles.headline: India-EU Green Alliance Sees Momentum with Joint Projects on Renewable Energy and Circular Economy
articles.explainer:
• The India-EU Green Alliance, established to deepen cooperation on climate action and sustainable development, reported significant progress in its joint projects.
• New collaborations include investments in large-scale renewable energy projects, particularly green hydrogen, and pilot initiatives for circular economy models in waste management.
• The alliance aims to facilitate technology transfer, capacity building, and policy dialogue to achieve shared climate goals and promote green growth.
• This partnership is crucial for India's ambitious climate targets and the EU's Green Deal objectives.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests.
prelimsPoints: Purpose of India-EU Green Alliance.
Key areas of cooperation (e.g., renewable energy, circular economy).
Significance of green hydrogen.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic importance of the India-EU Green Alliance in achieving global climate targets and fostering sustainable economic growth for both partners.
Discuss the potential of green hydrogen and circular economy models in India's transition to a low-carbon economy.
Evaluate the role of international partnerships in accelerating climate action and technology transfer.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and European Union with a green energy symbol.
articles.trivia: The European Union is the world's third-largest economy after China and the United States.

articles.headline: Environment Ministry Issues Revised Guidelines for Integrated Coastal Zone Management Plans
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEFCC) released revised guidelines for the preparation of Integrated Coastal Zone Management Plans (ICZMPs).
• The new guidelines aim to provide a more holistic framework for sustainable development of coastal areas, balancing conservation with economic activities.
• Emphasis is placed on climate change adaptation, disaster risk reduction, protection of critical coastal ecosystems (mangroves, coral reefs), and livelihood security for coastal communities.
• States and Union Territories are mandated to update their ICZMPs in line with these new directives.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Disaster and disaster management.
articles.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEFCC)
prelimsPoints: Ministry responsible for ICZMP guidelines.
Key objectives of ICZMP (conservation, livelihood, disaster reduction).
Importance of mangroves and coral reefs in coastal ecosystems.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges in implementing effective Integrated Coastal Zone Management Plans in India, considering developmental pressures and climate change impacts.
Analyze the ecological and socio-economic significance of coastal ecosystems and the need for their robust protection.
Evaluate the role of local communities in the success of coastal conservation and disaster preparedness efforts.
articles.imageDescription: A healthy mangrove forest along a coastline.
articles.trivia: India has a coastline of over 7,500 kilometers, including its island territories.

articles.headline: Indian Railways Expands 'Green Corridor' Network, Targets Net-Zero by 2030
articles.explainer:
• Indian Railways announced the expansion of its 'Green Corridor' network, aiming to electrify more routes and reduce its carbon footprint significantly.
• This initiative is part of the larger goal for Indian Railways to become a Net-Zero Carbon Emitter by 2030, a world-first for a large railway network.
• The expansion includes the deployment of solar power plants for traction energy and improved waste management systems at stations and workshops.
• The move is expected to enhance operational efficiency, reduce fuel costs, and contribute substantially to India's climate commitments.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
prelimsPoints: Target year for Indian Railways to become Net-Zero Carbon Emitter.
Key initiatives under 'Green Corridor' expansion (electrification, solar power).
Ministry responsible for Indian Railways.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of Indian Railways' Net-Zero target by 2030 for India's overall climate action and sustainable development goals.
Discuss the technological and financial challenges in achieving complete electrification and renewable energy integration in a vast railway network like India's.
Evaluate the broader impact of sustainable transport initiatives on environmental protection and economic efficiency.
articles.imageDescription: An electric train moving through a green landscape.
articles.trivia: Indian Railways is the fourth-largest railway network in the world.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Seeks Expert Opinion on Regulatory Framework for Artificial Intelligence in India
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India has initiated a suo motu (on its own motion) proceeding, seeking expert opinions on developing a comprehensive regulatory framework for Artificial Intelligence (AI) in India.
• The court noted the rapid advancements in AI and its potential impact on fundamental rights, data privacy, employment, and democratic processes.
• It invited submissions from government bodies, legal experts, AI ethicists, industry stakeholders, and civil society organizations.
• This move signals a proactive judicial approach to govern emerging technologies and ensure responsible AI development and deployment.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Role of civil services in a democracy.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: Nature of Supreme Court's action (suo motu).
Key concerns regarding AI identified by the court (fundamental rights, privacy, employment).
Stakeholders invited for consultation on AI regulation.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ethical, legal, and socio-economic challenges posed by rapid advancements in Artificial Intelligence and the necessity for a robust regulatory framework in India.
Analyze the potential role of the judiciary in shaping technology governance and balancing innovation with fundamental rights protection.
Examine different models of AI regulation adopted globally and suggest a suitable approach for India, considering its unique developmental context.
articles.imageDescription: A gavel striking a block with a backdrop of digital code.
articles.trivia: India is one of the leading countries in AI talent and adoption globally.

articles.headline: Government Unveils New Foreign Trade Policy 2025, Emphasizing Export Diversification and MSME Support
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Commerce and Industry launched the new Foreign Trade Policy (FTP) 2025, setting ambitious targets for export growth and diversification.
• The policy focuses on boosting exports from new sectors and products, expanding into non-traditional markets, and integrating Indian businesses into global value chains.
• Special provisions and incentives have been introduced for Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises (MSMEs) to encourage their participation in international trade.
• It also aims to streamline trade procedures, reduce transaction costs, and leverage e-commerce platforms for cross-border trade.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment.
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth.
prelimsPoints: Ministry responsible for Foreign Trade Policy.
Key objectives of FTP 2025 (export growth, MSME support, diversification).
Role of e-commerce in new FTP.
mainsPoints: Analyze the key features and objectives of the Foreign Trade Policy 2025 and its potential impact on India's export competitiveness and economic growth.
Discuss the challenges faced by Indian MSMEs in global trade and how the new policy seeks to address them.
Evaluate the role of technology and e-commerce in transforming India's foreign trade landscape.
articles.imageDescription: A container ship at a port with Indian flags, symbolizing international trade.
articles.trivia: India aims to achieve merchandise exports worth $1 trillion by 2030.

articles.headline: Quad Nations Hold High-Level Dialogue on Enhancing Maritime Security and HADR Capabilities
articles.explainer:
• Senior officials from the Quadrilateral Security Dialogue (Quad) nations – India, USA, Japan, and Australia – convened for a high-level dialogue on maritime security cooperation.
• The discussions focused on strengthening information sharing, improving coordination for Humanitarian Assistance and Disaster Relief (HADR) operations in the Indo-Pacific, and addressing challenges to a free and open Indo-Pacific.
• Emphasis was also placed on collaborating on critical maritime technologies and combating illegal, unreported, and unregulated (IUU) fishing.
• This dialogue reinforces the Quad's commitment to regional stability and adherence to international maritime law.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
India and its neighbourhood- relations.
prelimsPoints: Member countries of the Quad.
Key areas of cooperation discussed (maritime security, HADR, IUU fishing).
Geographical focus of Quad's activities.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolving role of the Quad in ensuring a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific region, particularly concerning maritime security challenges.
Analyze the significance of HADR cooperation among Quad nations in enhancing regional resilience to natural disasters.
Critically evaluate the implications of Quad's growing influence on India's strategic autonomy and its relations with other major powers.
articles.imageDescription: Naval ships from Quad nations participating in a joint exercise.
articles.trivia: The idea of the Quad was first mooted in 2007 by Japanese Prime Minister Shinzo Abe.

articles.headline: GST Council Meets to Discuss Tax Rationalization and Compliance Simplification Measures
articles.explainer:
• The Goods and Services Tax (GST) Council convened its 54th meeting to deliberate on crucial matters related to tax rationalization and simplification of compliance procedures.
• Key agenda items included reviewing the existing GST rate structure for certain goods and services, addressing inverted duty structure issues, and enhancing ease of doing business for taxpayers.
• Discussions also covered the integration of technology for better tax administration and resolving state-specific revenue concerns.
• The Council's decisions are anticipated to further streamline the indirect tax regime and promote economic growth.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
prelimsPoints: Composition of the GST Council.
Key functions of the GST Council.
Concept of inverted duty structure in GST.
mainsPoints: Discuss the impact of GST on India's economy since its implementation and the ongoing challenges in its administration and rate structure.
Analyze the importance of tax rationalization and compliance simplification for fostering a conducive business environment and enhancing tax revenue.
Evaluate the role of the GST Council as a federal institution in cooperative federalism and its effectiveness in resolving inter-state tax issues.
articles.imageDescription: A chart showing different GST slabs.
articles.trivia: The GST Council is chaired by the Union Finance Minister and includes state finance ministers as members.

articles.headline: Archaeologists Uncover New Insights into Indus Valley Civilization's Urban Planning and Water Management
articles.explainer:
• Recent archaeological excavations at a newly identified site in Gujarat have yielded significant discoveries about the advanced urban planning and sophisticated water management systems of the Indus Valley Civilization (IVC).
• Findings include evidence of intricate drainage systems, large public reservoirs, and multi-story structures, suggesting a highly organized society.
• The new site provides further proof of the extensive geographical spread of the IVC and its unique architectural and engineering prowess.
• These discoveries contribute to a deeper understanding of one of the world's earliest major civilizations.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.

History of the World will include events from 18th century such as industrial revolution, world wars, redrawal of national boundaries, colonization, decolonization, political philosophies like communism, capitalism, socialism etc.— their forms and effect on the society.
prelimsPoints: Key features of IVC urban planning and water management.
Location of new archaeological sites (Gujarat).
Other major IVC sites (Mohenjo-Daro, Harappa, Dholavira, Lothal).
mainsPoints: Discuss the salient features of the urban planning and water management systems of the Indus Valley Civilization and their relevance to contemporary challenges.
Analyze how new archaeological discoveries continually reshape our understanding of ancient Indian history and civilization.
Evaluate the factors contributing to the decline of the Indus Valley Civilization based on available evidence.
articles.imageDescription: Reconstruction of an Indus Valley Civilization city with its drainage system.
articles.trivia: The Great Bath of Mohenjo-Daro is one of the earliest public water tanks in the ancient world.

articles.headline: Scientists Discover New Amphibian Species in Western Ghats, Highlighting Biodiversity Hotspot Status
articles.explainer:
• A team of Indian scientists has announced the discovery of a new species of amphibian in the biodiversity-rich Western Ghats region.
• The discovery underscores the unique ecological significance of the Western Ghats, a UNESCO World Heritage Site and one of the world's 'hottest hotspots' of biodiversity.
• The new species, a type of frog, was identified through comprehensive genetic and morphological analysis.
• This finding emphasizes the urgent need for enhanced conservation efforts to protect the fragile ecosystems of the Western Ghats from habitat loss and climate change.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Biodiversity and its conservation.
prelimsPoints: Location of the discovery (Western Ghats).
Status of Western Ghats as a biodiversity hotspot and UNESCO site.
Importance of such discoveries for conservation.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological significance of the Western Ghats as a biodiversity hotspot and the major threats it faces.
Analyze the importance of discovering new species for scientific research and conservation strategies.
Suggest measures for effective biodiversity conservation in ecologically sensitive regions like the Western Ghats.
articles.imageDescription: A newly discovered frog species in its natural habitat in the Western Ghats.
articles.trivia: The Western Ghats is home to at least 325 globally threatened species.

articles.headline: India Records Record Growth in Digital Payments, Driving Financial Inclusion
articles.explainer:
• A new report released by the National Payments Corporation of India (NPCI) indicates a record surge in digital payment transactions across India in the last fiscal year.
• The growth is attributed to the widespread adoption of UPI, expansion of digital infrastructure in rural areas, and government initiatives promoting cashless transactions.
• This surge is significantly contributing to financial inclusion, bringing more unbanked and underbanked populations into the formal financial system.
• The report highlights India's position as a global leader in digital payment innovation and adoption.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: Organization releasing the digital payments report (NPCI).
Key driver of digital payment growth (UPI).
Impact of digital payments on financial inclusion.
mainsPoints: Discuss the transformative impact of digital payments, particularly UPI, on India's economy and financial inclusion landscape.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities associated with promoting a truly cashless economy in a diverse country like India.
Evaluate the role of government policies and technological innovation in accelerating digital payment adoption.
articles.imageDescription: People using smartphones for digital payments in a market.
articles.trivia: UPI transactions crossed 10 billion per month in 2023.

articles.headline: Largest Floating Solar Energy Park in India Inaugurated in Southern State
articles.explainer:
• India's largest floating solar energy park was officially inaugurated in a southern state, marking a significant milestone in the country's renewable energy journey.
• The park, with a capacity of 500 MW, is built over a large reservoir and is expected to power thousands of homes while reducing carbon emissions.
• Floating solar technology minimizes land use, reduces water evaporation from reservoirs, and improves panel efficiency due to cooler temperatures.
• This project aligns with India's ambitious target of achieving 500 GW of renewable energy capacity by 2030.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
articles.source: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE)
prelimsPoints: Concept of floating solar energy.
Benefits of floating solar technology (land use, water evaporation, efficiency).
India's renewable energy target for 2030.
mainsPoints: Discuss the advantages and challenges of adopting floating solar technology as a key component of India's renewable energy strategy.
Analyze how large-scale renewable energy projects contribute to India's climate commitments and energy security.
Evaluate the environmental and socio-economic impacts of such infrastructure projects on local communities and ecosystems.
articles.imageDescription: A vast expanse of solar panels floating on a reservoir.
articles.trivia: China currently has the largest operational floating solar farm in the world.

articles.headline: Union Government Rolls Out National Tourism Policy 2025 to Boost Sustainable Tourism and Rural Development
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Tourism launched the National Tourism Policy 2025, aiming to position India as a premier global tourist destination.
• The policy focuses on five key pillars: sustainable tourism, digital transformation, destination management, human resource development, and promoting rural and cultural tourism.
• It seeks to enhance infrastructure, improve connectivity, ensure safety and security for tourists, and create employment opportunities, especially in remote areas.
• The policy emphasizes community participation and responsible tourism practices to preserve India's natural and cultural heritage.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Salient features of Indian Society, Diversity of India.
prelimsPoints: Ministry responsible for National Tourism Policy.
Key pillars of National Tourism Policy 2025 (e.g., sustainable, rural, digital).
Benefits of tourism (employment, heritage preservation).
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential of tourism, particularly rural and cultural tourism, in driving socio-economic development and preserving heritage in India.
Analyze the challenges faced by India's tourism sector and how the National Tourism Policy 2025 attempts to address them.
Evaluate the concept of sustainable tourism and its importance in balancing economic growth with environmental and cultural preservation.
articles.imageDescription: Tourists visiting a historical monument in rural India.
articles.trivia: India's tourism sector contributed approximately 5.8% to the country's GDP in 2023.

articles.headline: Namami Gange Mission Enters Phase 3 with Focus on Tributary Cleaning and Public Participation
articles.explainer:
• The National Mission for Clean Ganga (NMCG) announced the commencement of Phase 3 of the Namami Gange program, a flagship initiative for rejuvenating the River Ganga.
• This new phase will expand its scope to include major tributaries of the Ganga, emphasizing comprehensive pollution abatement measures for Yamuna, Ramganga, and Damodar.
• A significant focus will be placed on enhancing public participation through community-led initiatives and awareness campaigns for sustainable river management.
• The mission aims for a 'Nirmal Ganga' (Clean Ganga) and 'Aviral Ganga' (Flowing Ganga) by integrating technological solutions and robust monitoring systems.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: Implementing agency of Namami Gange (NMCG).
New focus of Phase 3 (tributaries, public participation).
Key objectives of the mission (Nirmal Ganga, Aviral Ganga).
mainsPoints: Critically evaluate the progress and challenges of the Namami Gange Mission since its inception, particularly in achieving 'Nirmal' and 'Aviral' Ganga.
Discuss the importance of including tributaries and fostering public participation for holistic river basin management and rejuvenation.
Analyze the multi-faceted approach required for river cleaning, encompassing industrial, municipal, and agricultural pollution sources.
articles.imageDescription: A clean stretch of the River Ganga with ghats in the background.
articles.trivia: The Ganga river basin is the most populous river basin in the world.

highlights: India declares three-day state mourning for Pope Francis, emphasizing global diplomatic respect.
National Cyber Security Strategy 2025 unveiled, focusing on critical infrastructure protection and cyber resilience.
RBI expands Digital Rupee (e₹) pilot to include more banks and explore new use cases like cross-border payments.
ISRO begins preparations for Chandrayaan-4, an ambitious lunar sample return mission.
Supreme Court seeks expert opinion for a comprehensive regulatory framework for Artificial Intelligence in India.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to India's declaration of state mourning, consider the following statements:
1. State mourning is declared by the Ministry of Home Affairs.
2. During state mourning, the National Flag flies at half-mast on all government buildings nationwide.
3. State mourning is typically declared only for Indian dignitaries.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct. The Ministry of Home Affairs is responsible for issuing guidelines on state mourning. Statement 2 is correct. During state mourning, the National Flag is flown at half-mast as a mark of respect. Statement 3 is incorrect. While often declared for Indian dignitaries, state mourning can also be declared for foreign heads of state or prominent international figures, as seen in the case of Pope Francis.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following statements best describes the primary objective of ISRO's proposed Chandrayaan-4 mission?
options: (a) To establish a permanent human habitat on the Moon's South Pole.
(b) To conduct a comprehensive geological survey of the entire lunar surface.
(c) To achieve a lunar sample return, bringing back lunar soil and rock samples.
(d) To deploy a network of communication satellites around the Moon for future missions.
prelimsMocks.answer: (c) To achieve a lunar sample return, bringing back lunar soil and rock samples.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The article clearly states that Chandrayaan-4 aims to achieve a lunar sample return, bringing back lunar soil and rock samples for scientific analysis on Earth. While other options might be future goals, they are not the primary objective of this specific mission.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the National Cyber Security Strategy (NCSS) 2025:
1. It is launched by the Ministry of Defence.
2. It primarily focuses on strengthening cyber resilience for critical information infrastructure.
3. It emphasizes international collaboration to combat cyber threats.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect; the NCSS 2025 is launched by the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY). Statement 2 is correct, as the strategy emphasizes enhanced protection for critical information infrastructure. Statement 3 is also correct, as it aims to promote international collaboration to combat rising cyber threats.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following is NOT a stated key focus area for the new National Health Policy (NHP) currently under consultation?
options: (d) Ensuring equitable access to quality healthcare
prelimsMocks.explanation: The article mentions strengthening primary healthcare, integrating digital health solutions, and ensuring equitable access to quality healthcare as key focus areas for the new NHP. While medical tourism might be a related aspect of health policy, promoting it exclusively is not listed as a primary focus; the policy aims at universal healthcare for all citizens.

prelimsMocks.question: What is the primary benefit of 'Floating Solar Energy Parks' as highlighted in the news?
options: (a) They are significantly cheaper to build than land-based solar farms.
(b) They require no maintenance once installed.
(c) They minimize land use and reduce water evaporation from reservoirs.
(d) They can generate electricity during both day and night.
prelimsMocks.answer: (c) They minimize land use and reduce water evaporation from reservoirs.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The article states that floating solar technology 'minimizes land use, reduces water evaporation from reservoirs, and improves panel efficiency due to cooler temperatures.' Options (a) and (b) are generally not true, and (d) is incorrect as solar panels only generate electricity during the day.

mainsMocks.question: The passing of a global spiritual leader and India's response through state mourning reflects its evolving diplomatic posture and soft power. Discuss how such gestures contribute to India's international standing and its role in global interfaith dialogue. (150 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: India and its neighbourhood- relations; Important International institutions, agencies and fora; Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the event of Pope Francis's death and India's state mourning. Discuss the concept of soft power and how such gestures align with India's civilizational values and pluralistic ethos. Elaborate on how it strengthens diplomatic ties, showcases India's respect for global leaders, and reinforces its image as a responsible global actor. Mention its contribution to interfaith harmony and dialogue.

mainsMocks.question: India's push for a Central Bank Digital Currency (CBDC) like the Digital Rupee is a significant step towards modernizing its financial infrastructure. Analyze the potential benefits and challenges of CBDC adoption in India, particularly concerning financial inclusion, monetary policy, and cybersecurity. (250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment; Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the Digital Rupee and its pilot expansion. Discuss benefits: enhanced financial inclusion (especially for unbanked), faster and cheaper transactions, reduced cash dependency, improved monetary policy transmission, reduced illicit financial flows. Discuss challenges: cybersecurity risks, data privacy concerns, potential impact on commercial banks, technological literacy gaps, infrastructure requirements, and managing public perception. Conclude on the need for careful implementation and regulatory oversight.

mainsMocks.question: The Supreme Court's proactive move to seek expert opinion on AI regulation highlights the complex challenges posed by emerging technologies. Critically examine the ethical, legal, and socio-economic implications of Artificial Intelligence and the necessity for a robust regulatory framework in India. (250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation; GS3: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
mainsMocks.approach: Begin by acknowledging the rapid growth of AI and the Supreme Court's intervention. Discuss ethical implications: algorithmic bias, fairness, accountability, transparency, autonomous decision-making. Legal implications: data privacy, intellectual property, liability for AI errors, impact on fundamental rights. Socio-economic implications: job displacement, wealth concentration, digital divide, surveillance. Argue for a robust regulatory framework that balances innovation with protection of rights, promotes ethical AI development, ensures data security, and addresses societal impacts. Mention the need for a multi-stakeholder approach.

quickBytes.text: India declared three-day state mourning for Pope Francis, from April 21-23, 2025.
Tilakdhari Singh, veteran politician and former MP, passed away at 87.
National Cyber Security Strategy 2025 launched by MeitY, focuses on Critical Information Infrastructure.
RBI expands Digital Rupee (e₹) pilot, exploring cross-border and programmable payments.
ISRO initiated preparatory work for Chandrayaan-4, targeting a lunar sample return mission.
Supreme Court seeks expert opinion on AI regulatory framework, highlighting fundamental rights concerns.
New Foreign Trade Policy 2025 unveiled, emphasizing export diversification and MSME support.
Indian Railways aims to become Net-Zero Carbon Emitter by 2030, expanding 'Green Corridor' network.
Namami Gange Mission enters Phase 3, focusing on tributary cleaning and public participation.
The Western Ghats is a UNESCO World Heritage Site and one of the world's 'hottest hotspots' of biodiversity.

GS1.headline: Archaeologists Uncover New Insights into Indus Valley Civilization's Urban Planning and Water Management
GS1.explainer:
• Recent archaeological excavations at a newly identified site in Gujarat have yielded significant discoveries about the advanced urban planning and sophisticated water management systems of the Indus Valley Civilization (IVC).
• Findings include evidence of intricate drainage systems, large public reservoirs, and multi-story structures, suggesting a highly organized society.
• The new site provides further proof of the extensive geographical spread of the IVC and its unique architectural and engineering prowess.
• These discoveries contribute to a deeper understanding of one of the world's earliest major civilizations.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.

History of the World will include events from 18th century such as industrial revolution, world wars, redrawal of national boundaries, colonization, decolonization, political philosophies like communism, capitalism, socialism etc.— their forms and effect on the society.
prelimsPoints: Key features of IVC urban planning and water management.
Location of new archaeological sites (Gujarat).
Other major IVC sites (Mohenjo-Daro, Harappa, Dholavira, Lothal).
mainsPoints: Discuss the salient features of the urban planning and water management systems of the Indus Valley Civilization and their relevance to contemporary challenges.
Analyze how new archaeological discoveries continually reshape our understanding of ancient Indian history and civilization.
Evaluate the factors contributing to the decline of the Indus Valley Civilization based on available evidence.
GS1.imageDescription: Reconstruction of an Indus Valley Civilization city with its drainage system.
GS1.trivia: The Great Bath of Mohenjo-Daro is one of the earliest public water tanks in the ancient world.

GS1.headline: Union Government Rolls Out National Tourism Policy 2025 to Boost Sustainable Tourism and Rural Development
GS1.explainer:
• The Ministry of Tourism launched the National Tourism Policy 2025, aiming to position India as a premier global tourist destination.
• The policy focuses on five key pillars: sustainable tourism, digital transformation, destination management, human resource development, and promoting rural and cultural tourism.
• It seeks to enhance infrastructure, improve connectivity, ensure safety and security for tourists, and create employment opportunities, especially in remote areas.
• The policy emphasizes community participation and responsible tourism practices to preserve India's natural and cultural heritage.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Salient features of Indian Society, Diversity of India.
prelimsPoints: Ministry responsible for National Tourism Policy.
Key pillars of National Tourism Policy 2025 (e.g., sustainable, rural, digital).
Benefits of tourism (employment, heritage preservation).
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential of tourism, particularly rural and cultural tourism, in driving socio-economic development and preserving heritage in India.
Analyze the challenges faced by India's tourism sector and how the National Tourism Policy 2025 attempts to address them.
Evaluate the concept of sustainable tourism and its importance in balancing economic growth with environmental and cultural preservation.
GS1.imageDescription: Tourists visiting a historical monument in rural India.
GS1.trivia: India's tourism sector contributed approximately 5.8% to the country's GDP in 2023.

GS2.headline: India Declares Three-Day State Mourning Following Demise of Pope Francis
GS2.explainer:
• The Central Government announced a three-day period of state mourning across India from April 21st to 23rd, 2025, as a mark of respect for the passing of His Holiness Pope Francis.
• Pope Francis, the head of the Catholic Church and sovereign of the Vatican City State, passed away at the age of 88.
• During this period, the National Flag will fly at half-mast on all government buildings nationwide, and there will be no official entertainment.
• The declaration underscores India's respect for global spiritual leaders and its diplomatic relations with the Vatican, a significant global institution.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighbourhood- relations
Important International institutions, agencies and fora
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: Duration of state mourning declared by India.
Significance of state mourning as a diplomatic gesture.
Role of Pope Francis as head of Catholic Church and Vatican City State.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of India declaring state mourning for a global religious leader in the context of its soft power and diplomatic outreach.
Analyze the historical and contemporary relevance of India's relations with the Vatican and its role in international interfaith dialogue.
Examine the protocols and implications of state mourning in India and internationally.
GS2.imageDescription: Indian National Flag flying at half-mast over a government building.
GS2.trivia: The Vatican City is the smallest independent state in the world, both in area and population.

GS2.headline: Veteran Politician Tilakdhari Singh Passes Away at 87
GS2.explainer:
• Tilakdhari Singh, a veteran politician and former Member of Parliament, passed away at the age of 87.
• Known for his dedicated service to public life, Singh had a long and impactful career spanning several decades.
• He was recognized for his contributions to rural development and social justice initiatives in his constituency.
• His demise marks the end of an era for regional politics, with many leaders expressing condolences.
gsSubTopics: Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these.
Role of political parties in a democracy.
prelimsPoints: Tilakdhari Singh's political career and key roles held.
General awareness of significant political personalities.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolving role of veteran politicians in contemporary Indian democracy.
Analyze the impact of individual political leaders on regional development and social policies.
GS2.trivia: The average tenure of a Member of Parliament (Lok Sabha) is five years.

GS2.headline: Government Initiates Consultations for New National Health Policy Aimed at Universal Healthcare
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare has begun nationwide consultations for a new National Health Policy (NHP).
• The proposed policy aims to consolidate gains from previous policies and address emerging health challenges, including non-communicable diseases and pandemic preparedness.
• Key focus areas include strengthening primary healthcare, integrating digital health solutions, and ensuring equitable access to quality healthcare for all citizens.
• Stakeholders from medical professionals, public health experts, civil society, and state governments are invited to contribute.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
GS2.source: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
prelimsPoints: Ministry responsible for the National Health Policy.
Key objectives of the new NHP (e.g., universal healthcare, NCDs, digital health).
Stakeholders involved in the consultation process.
mainsPoints: Evaluate the effectiveness of previous National Health Policies in India and discuss the imperative for a new policy in the current socio-economic and epidemiological context.
Analyze the challenges in achieving universal healthcare access in India, particularly concerning funding, infrastructure, and human resources.
Discuss the role of digital health solutions and primary healthcare strengthening in improving health outcomes across the country.
GS2.imageDescription: Doctors and patients in a modern primary healthcare center.
GS2.trivia: India's first National Health Policy was formulated in 1983.

GS2.headline: India-EU Green Alliance Sees Momentum with Joint Projects on Renewable Energy and Circular Economy
GS2.explainer:
• The India-EU Green Alliance, established to deepen cooperation on climate action and sustainable development, reported significant progress in its joint projects.
• New collaborations include investments in large-scale renewable energy projects, particularly green hydrogen, and pilot initiatives for circular economy models in waste management.
• The alliance aims to facilitate technology transfer, capacity building, and policy dialogue to achieve shared climate goals and promote green growth.
• This partnership is crucial for India's ambitious climate targets and the EU's Green Deal objectives.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests.
prelimsPoints: Purpose of India-EU Green Alliance.
Key areas of cooperation (e.g., renewable energy, circular economy).
Significance of green hydrogen.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic importance of the India-EU Green Alliance in achieving global climate targets and fostering sustainable economic growth for both partners.
Discuss the potential of green hydrogen and circular economy models in India's transition to a low-carbon economy.
Evaluate the role of international partnerships in accelerating climate action and technology transfer.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India and European Union with a green energy symbol.
GS2.trivia: The European Union is the world's third-largest economy after China and the United States.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Seeks Expert Opinion on Regulatory Framework for Artificial Intelligence in India
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India has initiated a suo motu (on its own motion) proceeding, seeking expert opinions on developing a comprehensive regulatory framework for Artificial Intelligence (AI) in India.
• The court noted the rapid advancements in AI and its potential impact on fundamental rights, data privacy, employment, and democratic processes.
• It invited submissions from government bodies, legal experts, AI ethicists, industry stakeholders, and civil society organizations.
• This move signals a proactive judicial approach to govern emerging technologies and ensure responsible AI development and deployment.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Role of civil services in a democracy.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: Nature of Supreme Court's action (suo motu).
Key concerns regarding AI identified by the court (fundamental rights, privacy, employment).
Stakeholders invited for consultation on AI regulation.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ethical, legal, and socio-economic challenges posed by rapid advancements in Artificial Intelligence and the necessity for a robust regulatory framework in India.
Analyze the potential role of the judiciary in shaping technology governance and balancing innovation with fundamental rights protection.
Examine different models of AI regulation adopted globally and suggest a suitable approach for India, considering its unique developmental context.
GS2.imageDescription: A gavel striking a block with a backdrop of digital code.
GS2.trivia: India is one of the leading countries in AI talent and adoption globally.

GS2.headline: Quad Nations Hold High-Level Dialogue on Enhancing Maritime Security and HADR Capabilities
GS2.explainer:
• Senior officials from the Quadrilateral Security Dialogue (Quad) nations – India, USA, Japan, and Australia – convened for a high-level dialogue on maritime security cooperation.
• The discussions focused on strengthening information sharing, improving coordination for Humanitarian Assistance and Disaster Relief (HADR) operations in the Indo-Pacific, and addressing challenges to a free and open Indo-Pacific.
• Emphasis was also placed on collaborating on critical maritime technologies and combating illegal, unreported, and unregulated (IUU) fishing.
• This dialogue reinforces the Quad's commitment to regional stability and adherence to international maritime law.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
India and its neighbourhood- relations.
prelimsPoints: Member countries of the Quad.
Key areas of cooperation discussed (maritime security, HADR, IUU fishing).
Geographical focus of Quad's activities.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolving role of the Quad in ensuring a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific region, particularly concerning maritime security challenges.
Analyze the significance of HADR cooperation among Quad nations in enhancing regional resilience to natural disasters.
Critically evaluate the implications of Quad's growing influence on India's strategic autonomy and its relations with other major powers.
GS2.imageDescription: Naval ships from Quad nations participating in a joint exercise.
GS2.trivia: The idea of the Quad was first mooted in 2007 by Japanese Prime Minister Shinzo Abe.

GS3.headline: India Unveils National Cyber Security Strategy 2025 Focusing on Critical Infrastructure Protection
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) launched the National Cyber Security Strategy (NCSS) 2025.
• The strategy emphasizes enhanced protection for critical information infrastructure (CII) across sectors like energy, finance, and telecommunications.
• Key pillars include strengthening cyber resilience, fostering innovation in cyber technologies, and developing a skilled cybersecurity workforce.
• It aims to promote public-private partnerships and international collaboration to combat rising cyber threats.
gsSubTopics: Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges, basics of cyber security
Money-laundering and its prevention.
GS3.source: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY)
prelimsPoints: Implementing agency of NCSS 2025.
Key focus areas of NCSS 2025 (e.g., CII protection, skill development).
Importance of international collaboration in cybersecurity.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the provisions of the National Cyber Security Strategy 2025 and its potential effectiveness in safeguarding India's digital assets.
Discuss the challenges in protecting Critical Information Infrastructure from state-sponsored and non-state cyber actors.
Evaluate the role of public-private partnerships in enhancing national cybersecurity capabilities.
GS3.imageDescription: Digital representation of interconnected networks and a shield symbol.
GS3.trivia: India ranks among the top 10 countries most targeted by cyberattacks globally.

GS3.headline: RBI Expands Digital Rupee (e₹) Pilot to Include More Banks and Use Cases
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) announced a significant expansion of its Central Bank Digital Currency (CBDC) pilot project for both wholesale (e₹-W) and retail (e₹-R) segments.
• The expansion will include additional public and private sector banks and explore new use cases, such as cross-border payments and programmable payments.
• The move aims to gather more comprehensive data on the operational efficiency, technological resilience, and user adoption of the digital currency.
• This initiative is part of RBI's broader strategy to modernize India's payment infrastructure and enhance financial inclusion.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: Purpose of RBI's Digital Rupee pilot project.
Distinction between wholesale and retail CBDC.
Potential new use cases for Digital Rupee (e.g., cross-border, programmable payments).
mainsPoints: Assess the potential benefits and challenges of a Central Bank Digital Currency (CBDC) in India, particularly concerning financial stability and monetary policy transmission.
Discuss how the Digital Rupee could impact traditional banking systems and digital payment ecosystems in India.
Examine the technological underpinnings of CBDC and its implications for data privacy and cybersecurity.
GS3.imageDescription: Digital representation of Indian Rupee symbol with blockchain elements.
GS3.trivia: Globally, over 100 countries are exploring or developing their own CBDCs.

GS3.headline: ISRO Begins Preparations for Chandrayaan-4 Mission, Eyeing Lunar Sample Return
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) has initiated preliminary work for its ambitious Chandrayaan-4 mission, slated for launch in the late 2020s.
• This mission aims to achieve a lunar sample return, bringing back precious lunar soil and rock samples for scientific analysis on Earth.
• Chandrayaan-4 is envisioned as a multi-module mission, potentially involving a lander, rover, and an ascent vehicle to collect and return samples.
• This mission builds upon the successes of Chandrayaan-1, Chandrayaan-2, and Chandrayaan-3, further solidifying India's capabilities in lunar exploration.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of Space.
prelimsPoints: Primary objective of Chandrayaan-4 (lunar sample return).
Sequence of Chandrayaan missions (1, 2, 3, 4).
Key components of a sample return mission.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of lunar sample return missions for scientific research and national prestige in space exploration.
Analyze the technological challenges ISRO might face in executing a lunar sample return mission and its implications for future deep-space endeavors.
Evaluate India's position in global space exploration, considering its lunar missions and future ambitions.
GS3.imageDescription: Artistic rendering of a spacecraft collecting samples on the lunar surface.
GS3.trivia: Only three countries (USA, Russia/USSR, China) have successfully conducted lunar sample return missions so far.

GS3.headline: Environment Ministry Issues Revised Guidelines for Integrated Coastal Zone Management Plans
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEFCC) released revised guidelines for the preparation of Integrated Coastal Zone Management Plans (ICZMPs).
• The new guidelines aim to provide a more holistic framework for sustainable development of coastal areas, balancing conservation with economic activities.
• Emphasis is placed on climate change adaptation, disaster risk reduction, protection of critical coastal ecosystems (mangroves, coral reefs), and livelihood security for coastal communities.
• States and Union Territories are mandated to update their ICZMPs in line with these new directives.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Disaster and disaster management.
GS3.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEFCC)
prelimsPoints: Ministry responsible for ICZMP guidelines.
Key objectives of ICZMP (conservation, livelihood, disaster reduction).
Importance of mangroves and coral reefs in coastal ecosystems.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges in implementing effective Integrated Coastal Zone Management Plans in India, considering developmental pressures and climate change impacts.
Analyze the ecological and socio-economic significance of coastal ecosystems and the need for their robust protection.
Evaluate the role of local communities in the success of coastal conservation and disaster preparedness efforts.
GS3.imageDescription: A healthy mangrove forest along a coastline.
GS3.trivia: India has a coastline of over 7,500 kilometers, including its island territories.

GS3.headline: Indian Railways Expands 'Green Corridor' Network, Targets Net-Zero by 2030
GS3.explainer:
• Indian Railways announced the expansion of its 'Green Corridor' network, aiming to electrify more routes and reduce its carbon footprint significantly.
• This initiative is part of the larger goal for Indian Railways to become a Net-Zero Carbon Emitter by 2030, a world-first for a large railway network.
• The expansion includes the deployment of solar power plants for traction energy and improved waste management systems at stations and workshops.
• The move is expected to enhance operational efficiency, reduce fuel costs, and contribute substantially to India's climate commitments.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
prelimsPoints: Target year for Indian Railways to become Net-Zero Carbon Emitter.
Key initiatives under 'Green Corridor' expansion (electrification, solar power).
Ministry responsible for Indian Railways.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of Indian Railways' Net-Zero target by 2030 for India's overall climate action and sustainable development goals.
Discuss the technological and financial challenges in achieving complete electrification and renewable energy integration in a vast railway network like India's.
Evaluate the broader impact of sustainable transport initiatives on environmental protection and economic efficiency.
GS3.imageDescription: An electric train moving through a green landscape.
GS3.trivia: Indian Railways is the fourth-largest railway network in the world.

GS3.headline: Government Unveils New Foreign Trade Policy 2025, Emphasizing Export Diversification and MSME Support
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Commerce and Industry launched the new Foreign Trade Policy (FTP) 2025, setting ambitious targets for export growth and diversification.
• The policy focuses on boosting exports from new sectors and products, expanding into non-traditional markets, and integrating Indian businesses into global value chains.
• Special provisions and incentives have been introduced for Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises (MSMEs) to encourage their participation in international trade.
• It also aims to streamline trade procedures, reduce transaction costs, and leverage e-commerce platforms for cross-border trade.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment.
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth.
prelimsPoints: Ministry responsible for Foreign Trade Policy.
Key objectives of FTP 2025 (export growth, MSME support, diversification).
Role of e-commerce in new FTP.
mainsPoints: Analyze the key features and objectives of the Foreign Trade Policy 2025 and its potential impact on India's export competitiveness and economic growth.
Discuss the challenges faced by Indian MSMEs in global trade and how the new policy seeks to address them.
Evaluate the role of technology and e-commerce in transforming India's foreign trade landscape.
GS3.imageDescription: A container ship at a port with Indian flags, symbolizing international trade.
GS3.trivia: India aims to achieve merchandise exports worth $1 trillion by 2030.

GS3.headline: GST Council Meets to Discuss Tax Rationalization and Compliance Simplification Measures
GS3.explainer:
• The Goods and Services Tax (GST) Council convened its 54th meeting to deliberate on crucial matters related to tax rationalization and simplification of compliance procedures.
• Key agenda items included reviewing the existing GST rate structure for certain goods and services, addressing inverted duty structure issues, and enhancing ease of doing business for taxpayers.
• Discussions also covered the integration of technology for better tax administration and resolving state-specific revenue concerns.
• The Council's decisions are anticipated to further streamline the indirect tax regime and promote economic growth.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
prelimsPoints: Composition of the GST Council.
Key functions of the GST Council.
Concept of inverted duty structure in GST.
mainsPoints: Discuss the impact of GST on India's economy since its implementation and the ongoing challenges in its administration and rate structure.
Analyze the importance of tax rationalization and compliance simplification for fostering a conducive business environment and enhancing tax revenue.
Evaluate the role of the GST Council as a federal institution in cooperative federalism and its effectiveness in resolving inter-state tax issues.
GS3.imageDescription: A chart showing different GST slabs.
GS3.trivia: The GST Council is chaired by the Union Finance Minister and includes state finance ministers as members.

GS3.headline: Scientists Discover New Amphibian Species in Western Ghats, Highlighting Biodiversity Hotspot Status
GS3.explainer:
• A team of Indian scientists has announced the discovery of a new species of amphibian in the biodiversity-rich Western Ghats region.
• The discovery underscores the unique ecological significance of the Western Ghats, a UNESCO World Heritage Site and one of the world's 'hottest hotspots' of biodiversity.
• The new species, a type of frog, was identified through comprehensive genetic and morphological analysis.
• This finding emphasizes the urgent need for enhanced conservation efforts to protect the fragile ecosystems of the Western Ghats from habitat loss and climate change.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Biodiversity and its conservation.
prelimsPoints: Location of the discovery (Western Ghats).
Status of Western Ghats as a biodiversity hotspot and UNESCO site.
Importance of such discoveries for conservation.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological significance of the Western Ghats as a biodiversity hotspot and the major threats it faces.
Analyze the importance of discovering new species for scientific research and conservation strategies.
Suggest measures for effective biodiversity conservation in ecologically sensitive regions like the Western Ghats.
GS3.imageDescription: A newly discovered frog species in its natural habitat in the Western Ghats.
GS3.trivia: The Western Ghats is home to at least 325 globally threatened species.

GS3.headline: India Records Record Growth in Digital Payments, Driving Financial Inclusion
GS3.explainer:
• A new report released by the National Payments Corporation of India (NPCI) indicates a record surge in digital payment transactions across India in the last fiscal year.
• The growth is attributed to the widespread adoption of UPI, expansion of digital infrastructure in rural areas, and government initiatives promoting cashless transactions.
• This surge is significantly contributing to financial inclusion, bringing more unbanked and underbanked populations into the formal financial system.
• The report highlights India's position as a global leader in digital payment innovation and adoption.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: Organization releasing the digital payments report (NPCI).
Key driver of digital payment growth (UPI).
Impact of digital payments on financial inclusion.
mainsPoints: Discuss the transformative impact of digital payments, particularly UPI, on India's economy and financial inclusion landscape.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities associated with promoting a truly cashless economy in a diverse country like India.
Evaluate the role of government policies and technological innovation in accelerating digital payment adoption.
GS3.imageDescription: People using smartphones for digital payments in a market.
GS3.trivia: UPI transactions crossed 10 billion per month in 2023.

GS3.headline: Largest Floating Solar Energy Park in India Inaugurated in Southern State
GS3.explainer:
• India's largest floating solar energy park was officially inaugurated in a southern state, marking a significant milestone in the country's renewable energy journey.
• The park, with a capacity of 500 MW, is built over a large reservoir and is expected to power thousands of homes while reducing carbon emissions.
• Floating solar technology minimizes land use, reduces water evaporation from reservoirs, and improves panel efficiency due to cooler temperatures.
• This project aligns with India's ambitious target of achieving 500 GW of renewable energy capacity by 2030.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
GS3.source: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE)
prelimsPoints: Concept of floating solar energy.
Benefits of floating solar technology (land use, water evaporation, efficiency).
India's renewable energy target for 2030.
mainsPoints: Discuss the advantages and challenges of adopting floating solar technology as a key component of India's renewable energy strategy.
Analyze how large-scale renewable energy projects contribute to India's climate commitments and energy security.
Evaluate the environmental and socio-economic impacts of such infrastructure projects on local communities and ecosystems.
GS3.imageDescription: A vast expanse of solar panels floating on a reservoir.
GS3.trivia: China currently has the largest operational floating solar farm in the world.

GS3.headline: Namami Gange Mission Enters Phase 3 with Focus on Tributary Cleaning and Public Participation
GS3.explainer:
• The National Mission for Clean Ganga (NMCG) announced the commencement of Phase 3 of the Namami Gange program, a flagship initiative for rejuvenating the River Ganga.
• This new phase will expand its scope to include major tributaries of the Ganga, emphasizing comprehensive pollution abatement measures for Yamuna, Ramganga, and Damodar.
• A significant focus will be placed on enhancing public participation through community-led initiatives and awareness campaigns for sustainable river management.
• The mission aims for a 'Nirmal Ganga' (Clean Ganga) and 'Aviral Ganga' (Flowing Ganga) by integrating technological solutions and robust monitoring systems.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: Implementing agency of Namami Gange (NMCG).
New focus of Phase 3 (tributaries, public participation).
Key objectives of the mission (Nirmal Ganga, Aviral Ganga).
mainsPoints: Critically evaluate the progress and challenges of the Namami Gange Mission since its inception, particularly in achieving 'Nirmal' and 'Aviral' Ganga.
Discuss the importance of including tributaries and fostering public participation for holistic river basin management and rejuvenation.
Analyze the multi-faceted approach required for river cleaning, encompassing industrial, municipal, and agricultural pollution sources.
GS3.imageDescription: A clean stretch of the River Ganga with ghats in the background.
GS3.trivia: The Ganga river basin is the most populous river basin in the world.

sources: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY)
Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEFCC)
Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE)
